questions: Q1: What code changes were needed beyond the pre-existing implementation?
Q2: Do all CLI commands work correctly at runtime?
Q3: How many verification checks pass?

answers.answer: Three files needed fixing: get.ts, artifact.ts, and bundle.ts. All three expected the ticket detail API to return flat TicketDetail data, but the backend wraps it in { ticket: {...} }. Added TicketResponse wrapper type and unwrapped data.ticket in each file. This was a runtime bug discovered during Step 5 testing when tickets get crashed with 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading name)'.
evidence: src/tickets/get.ts line 23: added type TicketResponse = { ticket: TicketDetail }; line 26: changed to const data = (...) as TicketResponse; const ticket = data.ticket
src/tickets/artifact.ts line 10: added TicketResponse type; line 33: changed to const resp = (...) as TicketResponse; const ticket = resp.ticket
src/tickets/bundle.ts line 13: added TicketResponse type; line 33: changed to const resp = (...) as TicketResponse; const ticket = resp.ticket
Runtime test: curl GET /api/tickets/:id returns { ticket: {...} } wrapper confirmed via JSON.parse inspection

answers.answer: Yes. All 5 command groups route correctly (org, tickets, login, inspect, comments). Org commands (current/list/switch) work against the live API. Ticket discovery (list/latest/get) works with filters. Artifacts listing works. Bundle creates deterministic output. Error handling produces clear messages. The only partially verified command is tickets artifact (step artifact content reading) due to no step artifacts in the test database.
evidence: org current: displays PX Cracked org info
org list: shows 1 org with (current) marker
org switch: confirms switch to PX Cracked
tickets list --status-not-in DEPLOYED,FAILED: 0 excluded statuses in output
tickets get: shows full detail (title, branch, repos, description)
tickets artifacts: lists 2 repo artifacts for HLX-11
tickets bundle: creates ticket.json + manifest.json with correct fields
Error handling: 4 scenarios all exit 1 with clear messages

answers.answer: 14 of 15 total checks pass (11/12 helix-cli + 3/3 helix-global-server). CHK-10 (helix-cli artifact raw content read) is blocked because no step artifacts exist in the test database. The command code path runs correctly and hits the API, but raw content printing cannot be observed.
evidence: CHK-01 through CHK-09: pass (typecheck, build, org current/list/switch, tickets list/filter/get, artifacts)
CHK-10: blocked - no step artifacts in test DB, API returns 404
CHK-12: pass (all error cases exit 1 with messages)
Server CHK-01 through CHK-03: pass (typecheck, code presence, runtime filter)

statement: Fixed 3 files (get.ts, artifact.ts, bundle.ts) to unwrap the { ticket: {...} } API response wrapper that caused runtime crashes. All other pre-existing code was correct. TypeScript typecheck and build pass. 14 of 15 verification checks pass across both repos; CHK-10 blocked by test data limitations (no step artifacts in database).
